chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: the squatting problem was why MIME media types had the "standards tree".

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: Did we ever discuss anywhere what the definition of "recognised" should be?

chat.author: Eliot Lear
chat.text: the iesg keeps a list

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: Yes, extensively. That's the SDO registry.

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: I know about that list, but there's no definition for what qualifies as "recognised".

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: It's IESG judgement, and that can very as its membership changes.

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: No, it's "the IESG has decided that it's recognized".

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: so the IESG is responsible for documenting their policy.

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: They never have though.

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: which is not a new problem :-)

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: Having this be semi-vibes-based by the IESG isn't terrible, it avoids making it gameable

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: Interesting point.

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: The slop problem is real, but I can't see this working.  Maybe we should stop making human-readable labels.

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: That would solve the squatting problem.

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: Queue closed; 8 queued, 46 minutes to go, one other presentation.

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: +1 Harald on underestimating reference permanence

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: My mind is clearly not functioning well.  I filled in for Harald: "when two implementations [love each other very much]..."

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: Remote folks, can you hear Amanda?

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: I can hear her.

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: Thanks @Harald

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: What is the implication for registering an SDO?  Does that imply permanence?  Or do we still need a separate consideration of that?

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: Hi Carsten.  If the IESG removes them from the list, that would be effective for any requested actions after the removal.  Would you like that text in the document?

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: You can't really say why people want choices--I remember someone asking the SRI-NIC for a specific IP prefix because it matched their telephone number prefix.

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: Having the author not be allowed to suggest, say, the name of a new HTTP header field would be really weird.

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: Ted: I was thinking about reference permanence, which is somewhat independent of permanence of our recognition of the SDO.

chat.author: Rahul Gupta
chat.text: Even numeric codes sometimes have a textual feel. Say HTTP 420 error code.

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: I think that in an ideal world, update procedures would be used much more than they presently are. Most permanent references are in reality pointing at obsolete documents.

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: IMHO "Yes" on the 2119 question.

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: I'd rather have an obsolete document than no document

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: As long as it makes its obsolesence plain.

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: Harald: I think we need to make more obvious how the "change control" power conferred to registrants is intended to be exercised

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Carsten +1

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: 8126bis could open a registry of subpolicies, to be filled in by separate documents...

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: +1 no 2119 in IC sections (the section binds IANA and the registrant, not the implementer)

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: That's exactly my reasoning too.

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: If you want strong enforcement, assign a DE.

chat.author: Rahul Gupta
chat.text: Can a registry designer not at extra rules for DE for that specific situation?

chat.author: Murray Kucherawy
chat.text: Yes.

chat.author: Rahul Gupta
chat.text: At =add*
